description: A baked dessert made by soaking stale or day-old bread in a sweet custard, then baking it until the center is soft and the top is lightly browned. It is a classic way to use leftover bread.
preparation_steps: Cube or tear the bread and place it in a baking dish.
Pour the custard over the bread and let it soak.
Add any fruit, spice, or sauce elements if using.
Bake until set but still soft in the center.

health_advice.benefits: A practical way to use leftover bread and reduce food waste.
health_advice.risks: Dessert-style versions can be high in sugar and saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Reduce sugar, use moderate portions, and add fruit instead of heavier sauces when possible.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional versions are vegetarian. Vegan and gluten-free versions are possible with appropriate substitutions.
